observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Accurately reflect in the original budget column of the current year’s SCBAA the ending balances of previous year’s CO in accordance with IPSAS No. 24 and Section 322 of RA No. 7160; and
Strengthen validation procedures during the finalization of year-end reports by performing a reconciliation between the SAAOB and the SCBAA to ensure the accuracy and consistency of reported balances and to avoid the same oversight in the succeeding year.

We recommended the LCE direct the:
Supply Officer and MEO to timely submit at year-end the disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate prompt and proper recording of transactions; and
Municipal Accountant to withhold the applicable taxes on all accrued expenses and remit the same to the BIR within the prescribed period in compliance with RR No. 04-2024.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer–Designate to retrieve all relevant records and documents related to the untraced items, especially those pertaining to the demolished asset, to identify and properly account for these items in the PPE accounts; otherwise, if documents pertaining thereto could not be retrieved, the Municipal Engineer to estimate the carrying amount of the demolished asset taking into consideration the cost, age and condition of the said asset; and
Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjustments in the appropriate PPE account to derecognize the demolished structure.

We recommended the LCE to direct the:
BAC, through its BAC Secretariat, to prepare and send the Notification of Bid Submission to ensure transparency and accurate representation of the bids submitted.
BAC and its TWG to scrutinize the contractor’s submitted list of key personnel to be assigned to the contract and thoroughly examine it for any overlapping assignments. In cases where personnel are found to be concurrently assigned to multiple projects during the same period, require the contractor, through a written request, to replace the concerned personnel. Failure to comply should result in the disqualification of the contractor for non-compliance with the technical requirements of the procurement process.
Municipal Engineer to:
Implement standardized field inspection checklists and reporting templates to ensure that project signboards comply with the specifications in the POW; and
Develop and adopt a uniform costing template or guidelines for the preparation of project signboard estimates, in order to promote consistency and fairness, and to minimize discretion in cost estimation.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Supply Officer to:
maintain and periodically update SCs using the form prescribed under the NGAS Manual;
submit official copies of SSMI to the accounting office on a regular basis; and
coordinate with appropriate offices for the establishment of storage room to facilitate stocking of inventories instead of adapting the as-needed procurement basis.
Municipal Accountant to:
ensure timely recording of the inventories purchased in the books of accounts;
recognize the corresponding expenses monthly in the books of accounts based on the submitted SSMI, in accordance with the mandated perpetual inventory method;
apply the moving average method of costing inventories in compliance with Section 115 of the NGAS Manual; and
maintain and periodically update SLCs using the form prescribed in the NGAS Manual.
Inventory Committee to:
spearhead the conduct of semi-annual physical count of inventories; and
ensure that all inventories on hand as a result of the physical count, including those issued to the departments but not yet ultimately distributed to the employees as the end-users, are included in the RPCI.
Human Resources Management Officer to assess the manpower complement needed in the Supply Office and Accounting Office and augment employee thereat as necessary.
We also recommended that the LCE requires the appropriate key personnel—such as the Municipal Accountant, the Supply Officer, and members of the IC, among others—to attend seminars or training on inventory management to enhance their knowledge and ensure proper implementation of inventory procedures in accordance with applicable regulations and best practices.

We recommended the LCE discontinue the granting of additional cash advances to Municipal Treasurer who has outstanding or unliquidated cash advances, in accordance with existing accounting and auditing regulations, to ensure proper accountability and safeguard public funds.
We also recommended the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to assess the need to designate additional Disbursing Officer/s, subject to the availability of funds for bond premiums, otherwise strategically plan the timing and frequency of issuance of cash advances to the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MENRO-Designate, in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer, to provide training for the garbage collectors and other personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid wastes in compliance with Section 23 (b) of  RA No. 9003, otherwise consider the TESDA Garbage Collector NC I training program to equip them with the necessary skills on proper handling of waste and avoid exposure to possible health risks and injuries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen financial management on cash receipts by: 

strictly enforce the Collectors to remit daily; 


refrain from using the collections in augmenting cash advances for operating expenses; and


deposit the collections intact daily or on the next banking day to safeguard the fund from possible loss, misuse, or misappropriation and to ensure the availability of cash for its immediate use to implement essential PPAs.
observations.managementAction:
Collectors remit daily to the Municipal Treasurer as best as they could.


The Municipality has increased its Petty Cash Fund (PCF) for operating expenses to avoid using the collections in augmenting cash advances.


Stricter internal controls over cash management and implementing a regular monitoring system for timely remittances and setting clear deadlines to avoid delays.

In addition, establish control mechanisms in the monitoring and execution of plans to ensure that all PPAs are implemented as planned.

We have recommended that Management immediately rescind the contract and issue Notice to Terminate to the Contractor’s firm containing the necessary details provided in Section IV.2 of Annex I of RA 9184. Thereafter, resume the project in the earliest possible time which may be procured through Negotiated Procurement –Take-Over of Contracts as provided under Section IV. 7 of Annex I of the same law, to ensure that the associated gender issues will be addressed and expected benefits will be enjoyed by target recipient communities during CY 2020.
Further we recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Project Monitoring Committee strictly monitor on-going infrastructure projects and immediately take appropriate actions for issues determined as prescribed under RA 9184 to ensure that projects are completed as scheduled.
We have recommended that the Management judiciously review and ensure that the Performance Security issued by the contractor before contract signing is strictly in accordance with the required validity period under Section 39.4 of RA 9184 for the succeeding procurement of projects to ensure that the Municipality is duly secured in the event of non-compliance with the agreed upon contract. As such, disqualify the bidder for not meeting the requirements, forfeit his bid security, and initiate and complete the post-qualification process on the bidder with the second Lowest Calculated Bid/Highest Rated Bid until the LCRB or HRRB is determined for award as provided under Section 40 – Failure to Enter into Contract and Post Performance Security of the same law. Lastly, to take advantage of the Performance Bond in the event that compliance of the contractor during construction becomes unlikely.

We have recommended that the Management:
Require additional payment for the uncollected penalties during the CY 2019. Thereafter, strictly implement the penalty provisions of the current Tax Ordinance requiring assessment and collection of penalties for delayed payments of business taxes as additional income may be realized to fund the projects to be implemented, as well as to ensure earlier collection of business taxes; and
Require the submission of certified copy of Annual Income Tax Return to Business Taxpayers on the second quarter of the year; adjust the business tax and; collect the difference if there are discrepancies between the amount appearing in the ITR and gross sales declared in the business tax application form.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile these reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries.
Further, she committed to check that the balances of these reciprocal accounts through performing constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter errors or omissions and preparation of monthly reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts to ensure that the reciprocal accounts are always equal and are not included in the consolidated financial statements.  In this way, the financial statements will be accurately presented.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly implement the provisions of RA No. 9184 on all of its procurement.  It should judiciously prepare, maintain and update a Project Procurement Plan (PPMP) for each individual project to be consolidated in the Annual Procurement Plan (APP), which shall be the basis for procurement.
It is further recommended that the Municipality adopt public bidding as the general mode of procurement and resort only to alternative methods in highly exceptional cases provided under RA No. 9184.
The MSWD as the lead personnel in the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program coordinate with the Bids and Awards Committee and incorporate in the Purchase Request and PPMP the staggered delivery period.

We recommended and management positively agreed that aside from the initial water tests, they will see to it that periodic examination of water will be made.  Further, to ensure protection against contamination of water they will ask the Sangguniang Bayan through the initiative of the Municipal Health Officer to pass an ordinance on the Protection of Water Sources against contamination.

We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation and Management guaranteed that they will submit the required liquidation/utilization reports to donor agencies.  Further management to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in detail particularly the utilization.

We recommended and Management positively agreed to review their LDRRM Plan and incorporate projects or activities that would address the noted deficiencies for each of the categories of the minimum preparedness standards as provided under the NDPP.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive committed to direct the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection on completed projects after the one (1) year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if no defects and deficiencies were detected. Nevertheless, if construction defects and failures were detected upon inspection, then corresponding measures must be undertaken by the contractor to correct them.
Further, to oblige the contractor to post warranty security that will answer structural defects/failures that may be discovered from final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2 to ensure that the contractor will perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 (a) of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
Also, for the management to furnish the audit team copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed projects to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct:
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to submit complete list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year;
The Municipal Engineer to furnish the audit team copies of Contract Extensions and Suspension of Work, if there are any; and
The MPDC and Municipal Engineer to inform the audit team and Supervising Auditor that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted.

We recommended and the TWG agreed to our recommendation that in cases there would be simultaneous projects to be awarded to only one contractor, the BAC and TWG will require the contractor to augment its manpower who will supervise and handle in writing.

In view of the above, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Budget Officer and Acting Municipal Accountant to provide the Municipal Health Officer with fund balances regularly, PhilHealth and MHO – regular Budget;
Municipal Health Officer to prepare a fund utilization program with timeliness for review and approval of the Local Chief Executive to ensure efficient and timely utilization of PhilHealth - Primary Care Benefit fund; and
Coordinate with the Barangay Health Centers to determine their needs that can be funded by the PhilHealth – Primary Care Benefit fund in order to respond appropriately and timely to the need of the constituents covered by the health care package.

We recommended and Management agreed to collect the remaining balance from the NGO/PO through issuance of demand letter.  Further, for future loans to NGO/PO, we enjoin management to strictly monitor the implementation and collection of receivables.

We recommended that a timely implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program will start from a well-planned implementation in order to address malnutrition among day care children.  Prior to the opening of school year, with the MSWD Office as the proponent of the program, had at least gathered data such as expected number of enrollees, suggested menu for the 120 days supplementary feeding program and consolidate the same to facilitate the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan and PPMP for the said program hence ease procurement process.

We recommended and Management agreed to create WATSAN to be chaired by the Municipal Mayor.  Establish and develop capacity of Water and Sanitation (WATSAN) Councils and water user associations through coordination with DILG RO No. 1.  Further, the LGU to pass an ordinance through the recommendation made by the created WATSAN for the maintenance, operation and supervision and monitoring of water supply system to ensure sustainability of water supply and services.

We recommended and Management committed to ensure monitoring the full implementation of the projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to address the needs of the constituents such as access to improved health, social, agriculture and economic services.  Further, for the Municipal Development Council to review prior years’ unimplemented projects under the 20% development fund and revise their Annual Investment Plan which are directly supportive of the Philippine Development Plan and Public Investment Program.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reconcile these reciprocal accounts and make the necessary adjusting entries. Henceforth, she committed to check that the balances of these reciprocal accounts are always equal and are not included in the consolidated financial statements. In this way, the financial statements will be accurately presented.
observations.managementAction: To establish records of reciprocal accounts to identify the discrepancies.
observations.reason: Start tracing back records to identify the discrepancies discovered in order to come up with equal balances.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the LCE direct the Treasury Office to keep files of photocopied required documents and to properly fill-up the form for application of business permits inorder to validate the true and correct amounts assessed and collected.
observations.managementAction: To require all business owners to fill-up all information in the business application form.

observations.recommendation: The team have recommended and management promised to revisit the completed projects during the one (1) year defects liability period and issue the Final Certificate of Acceptance.  To compel the contractors to repair if there are defects due to use of inferior materials or to forfeit the Performance Security if there are defects noted.  Further, to require the contractor to issue or submit Warranty Security for Structural Defects.
observations.reason: Revisited the completed projects and issued Final Certificate of Acceptance. Compelled the contractors to repair any defects and required them to submit Warranty Security.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed to strictly comply to the above provision of R.A. No. 9184 to guarantee that manufacturing defects, if any, on the goods procured will be corrected by the supplier.
observations.reason: Required the contractors to submit Warranty Security.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer, prior to transferring or opening of account, must secure first a Resolution from the Sanggunian Bayan authorizing her and the Local Chief Executive to open a new account.
We have reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Accounting Unit maintain complete property ledger cards instead of subsidiary ledgers with details or breakdown.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to trace the details of the account Other Land Improvements.  We recommended further that adjusting entries be prepared to record the depreciation of the affected PPE accounts.
We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant committed to adjust the accounts Market; Hospitals and Health Centers; Government Equity and the corresponding Accumulated Depreciation.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause or order the Municipal Accountant to further look into previous years’ files in order to account for the said receivables.  If after all the possible means were exerted and the claims could not be accounted, request for write-off of the above receivables supported by above cited documents.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to release the aid to barangays the soonest possible time.
Further, for the Municipal Accountant to record as receivables upon release of the fund/aid to barangay and to revert the same upon validation and verification of liquidation report.
Lastly, in order to have assurance that the fund released was used for the purpose it was intended, the Municipal Accountant must require for the liquidation of the fund released to barangay.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to release the barangay shares from real property taxes to the different barangays within five (5) days after the end of each quarter and shall not be subjected to any lien or holdback for whatever purpose in order that all the shares from real property taxes could be utilized by the barangays for the benefits of their constituents.

We have recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to look into the details and documents related to the above payables to determine if they are no longer needed and to remit the same to the Bureau of Treasury.

We have recommended and management agreed that in order to have an assurance that the Building Code of the Philippines is properly enforced within the Municipality and in order to increase collection from building permit fees, the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Barangay Officials to enforce laws and ordinances applicable within the barangay and to coordinate with the Building Official when they notice that there are structures being built within their barangay.

We have recommended and management committed to review the Municipality’s Revenue Code and correct in order to align with the provisions to relevant laws, rules and regulations.
Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee and Sanggunian Bayan Members, must see to it that proposed and approved tax ordinance or revenue measure are consistent and aligned with the relevant laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended and the Acting Municipal Mayor committed that she will direct the Technical Working Group (TWG) to strictly observe and comply with the requirements expressed in Section 34.3 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 in the post-qualification to ensure that only bidders with the lowest calculated responsive bids are awarded the contracts.
We have recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee assured us that posted Performance Bond will completely bear all the essential information especially the date of issuance.

We have required the Municipal Engineer and BAC to justify the inconsistency of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) of the above project, however, as of this writing, we have not received any letter justifying for the inconsistency.

We have recommended that the BAC publish in the PhilGEPS the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed within the prescribed period as provided for in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

The audit team recommended for the Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to disclose on the face of the disbursement voucher and Obligation Slip any retention money deducted from progress payments, withhold the amount until final payment.
We further recommended the retention money be released only upon final acceptance of the works or upon submission by the contractor of a substitute in the form of either irrevocable standby letters of credit from a commercial bank, bank guaranty or surety bonds callable on demand, of amounts equivalent to the retention money, provided that the project is on schedule and is satisfactorily undertaken.

We have recommended for the municipal government to oblige the contractor to post warranty security which are properly filled out and bear the date of issuance and expiration. These will answer structural defects/failures that may be discovered from final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2 to ensure that the contractor will perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 (a) of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. Further, to ensure that the posted surety bonds contain all necessary details and information.

We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive committed to direct the municipal engineer to conduct detailed engineering prior to procurement of all infrastructure projects with reasonable accuracy to avoid variation/change orders and work suspensions which cause delayed completion of projects.

We have recommended and the BAC agreed to inform all the units/department that PPMP will be based on every quarter requirement and not on every semester.  Further, to plan and prepare the APP with an advance procurement actions in order to ensure that the goods are available prior or on the first day of the quarter.
We have recommended and the Local Chief Executive committed to require the BAC to comply with the requirements as provided by RA No. 9184 particularly the procurement of medicines.

The audit team recommended that a signboard or billboard as required by the above Circulars be posted for all PPAs of the municipality with the essential features/information to ensure transparency and encourage public participation to the activities of the municipality.
Further, for the agency to provide a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year and to notify the audit team on the posting of the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices for the purpose of validation and verification.

We have recommended that the municipality reflect in the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents the actual cost of the rice seeds for transparency.  Thereafter, collection from farmers be properly receipted and recorded for accountability.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to use the funds received for calamity intended to normalize the living conditions of the affected constituents.
Further, to guarantee the submission of liquidation reports to donor agencies.

We have recommended that the management stop the practice of paying in full amount the representation and transportation allowances of the SBMs whenever they incur absences during their regular sessions and require the refund of P108,400.00 from the concerned SBMs.

We recommended for the management to immediately settle the above suspension which was settled during at year-end. Henceforth, for the Municipal Accountant to require complete documentation of all financial transactions to avoid suspensions in the post-audit of accounts by the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality consider:

Integrating the recommendations of the DENR – Environmental Management Bureau in their 10-year SWM Plan; 

Encourage all barangays to have their own material recovery facilities; and


c.	Continue the best practice of enhancing awareness of the stakeholders on existing laws, rules and regulations on solid waste management and strictly observing and implementing the provisions thereof.
observations.managementAction:
Strictly comply with the DENR recommendations. 

To establish material recovery facility for every barangay.
observations.reason:
Complied earnestly with the rules and recommendations of DENR.

Every barangay has established their own material recovery facility.
